message.content:
This section (Page 4) outlines the visualization standards, quantitative market performance, and methodological boundaries for the Chinese mobile gaming industry, contextualizing the sector's shift from “recovery anxiety” to “strategic dormancy.” It supports retrieval for the following elements:
- **Topics & Entities:** Chinese mobile gaming market revenue and growth trajectories. Primary data entities include Diandian Data (点点数据), corporate financial reports, and industry experts.
- **Definitions & Scope:** Restricts statistical scope to mobile games launched in mainland China (excludes PC/console; multi-platform titles count only mobile data). Defines “revenue” strictly as total direct user consumption, explicitly excluding ad monetization and third-party top-ups. This definition governs all subsequent “China revenue” metrics in the report.
- **Dates & Timeframes:** Covers historical performance (2021–2024) and forecasted projections (2025–2028). Highlights 2025 as a critical forecast/current-year marker and notes potential data adjustments in 2026 reports.
- **Metrics & Data Points:** Tracks market revenue scale (亿元/100 million RMB) and annual growth rate (%). Key values: 2021 (2769.4亿元, negative growth), 2022 (3.21%), 2023 (10.74% peak), and 2028 projection (3059.2亿元, 1.83%).
- **Figures & Visual Conventions:** Features a combined bar/line chart. Blue bars denote annual revenue scale; cyan lines denote annual growth rates. A red dashed box annotation specifically marks 2025 data points to indicate current-year or critical forecast status. No tables are present.
- **Claims & Trends:** Asserts a post-2021 market contraction followed by resumed growth in 2022, a momentum peak in 2023, and a forecasted gradual deceleration into a stable, long-term growth trajectory through 2028.
- **Evidence Types & Methodology:** Structured time-series numerical data, quantitative visual evidence (charts/legends), and synthesized analytics derived from third-party data, financial disclosures, and expert interviews, processed via Diandian Data’s proprietary statistical model. No procedures are documented.
This summary enables precise retrieval for queries on chart legends, metric-to-visualization mappings, historical vs. forecasted performance, data scope limitations, and industry trend interpretations.

- **Definitions & Scope:** Restricts statistical scope to mobile games launched in mainland China (excludes PC/console; multi-platform titles count only mobile data). Defines “revenue” strictly as total direct user consumption, explicitly excluding ad monetization and third-party top-ups. This definition governs all subsequent “China revenue” metrics in the report.
- **Dates & Timeframes:** Covers historical performance (2021–2024) and forecasted projections (2025–2028). Highlights 2025 as a critical forecast/current-year marker and notes potential data adjustments in 2026 reports.
- **Metrics & Data Points:** Tracks market revenue scale (亿元/100 million RMB) and annual growth rate (%). Key values: 2021 (2769.4亿元, negative growth), 2022 (3.21%), 2023 (10.74% peak), and 2028 projection (3059.2亿元, 1.83%).
- **Figures & Visual Conventions:** Features a combined bar/line chart. Blue bars denote annual revenue scale; cyan lines denote annual growth rates. A red dashed box annotation specifically marks 2025 data points to indicate current-year or critical forecast status. No tables are present.
- **Claims & Trends:** Asserts a post-2021 market contraction followed by resumed growth in 2022, a momentum peak in 2023, and a forecasted gradual deceleration into a stable, long-term growth trajectory through 2028.
- **Evidence Types & Methodology:** Structured time-series numerical data, quantitative visual evidence (charts/legends), and synthesized analytics derived from third-party data, financial disclosures, and expert interviews, processed via Diandian Data’s proprietary statistical model. No procedures are documented.
This summary enables precise retrieval for queries on chart legends, metric-to-visualization mappings, historical vs. forecasted performance, data scope limitations, and industry trend interpretations.
